title: Scrap Yard
author: JSL EKOS
description: This module explains the purpose of the Scrap Yard, types of scrap used in stainless steel manufacturing, inspection procedures, segregation methods, storage practices, and charging preparation before steelmaking.
learningObjectives: Understand the role of the Scrap Yard.
Identify different types of scrap used in stainless steel production.
Learn scrap inspection and segregation procedures.
Understand scrap storage and handling practices.
Recognize the importance of scrap quality in steelmaking.

content.introduction: The Scrap Yard is the starting point of stainless steel production. It receives, inspects, segregates, stores, and prepares scrap before it is charged into the Electric Arc Furnace (EAF). Proper scrap management improves productivity, reduces contamination, and ensures consistent steel quality.
content.overview: Scrap received from different sources is inspected for quality, sorted according to grade, stored safely, and prepared according to production requirements before charging into the furnace.

typesOfScrap.name: Home Scrap
typesOfScrap.description: Scrap generated within the manufacturing plant.

typesOfScrap.name: Stainless Steel Scrap
typesOfScrap.description: High-quality stainless steel scrap used as the primary metallic charge.

typesOfScrap.name: Return Scrap
typesOfScrap.description: Rejected or unused material returned from production.

content.summary: The Scrap Yard plays a vital role in stainless steel manufacturing by ensuring that only properly inspected, segregated, and prepared scrap is supplied for steelmaking, directly influencing production efficiency and product quality.
